name: Christmas Island (Australia)

colors.symbolism: the sky and the Indian Ocean surrounding the island; recalls the blue of the Australian flag
the dense tropical rainforest covering most of the island
the island's mineral wealth (especially phosphate, the basis of its economy) and the gold-spotted plumage of the golden bosun bird; the yellow disc and bird
the seven-pointed stars of the Southern Cross

description: A flag divided diagonally from the upper hoist to the lower fly, with the fly-side triangle green and the hoist-side triangle blue; the blue (hoist) side carries the white Southern Cross in the upper-hoist quadrant, the green (fly) side carries the golden bosun bird in the upper-fly quadrant, and at the centre is a yellow disc charged with a green silhouette of the island.
history: The flag was originally designed by Tony Couch, who won a 1986 competition organised by the Christmas Island Assembly. It was used unofficially from 1986 and adopted as the official flag of the territory on Australia Day 2002 (26 January).

features.symbolism: the diagonal split represents the rainforests and land of the island (green) on one side and the sky and surrounding ocean (blue) on the other
Five seven-pointed stars in the configuration of the Southern Cross constellation, as on the Australian flag — recalling Christmas Island's status as an Australian external territory.
The golden bosun bird — a subspecies of white-tailed tropicbird endemic to Christmas Island and considered an emblem of the territory.

features.role: central yellow disc bearing a green map of the island
features.symbolism: A yellow disc charged with a green silhouette of the island, placed at the centre of the flag where the blue and green halves meet — symbolising Christmas Island itself, surrounded by the colours of sky/sea and forest.
